principle.name: Plan Before Implementation
principle.description: Never skip directly to writing code. Always create a written plan first that the user can review and approve.
principle.rationale: Rushed plans lead to rework, missed edge cases, and solutions that don't match user intent.

principle.name: Write Plans to Files
principle.description: Every plan must be saved as a markdown document in the docs/ directory. Verbal plans alone are insufficient.
principle.rationale: File-based plans can be referenced during implementation, shared with team members, and tracked over time.

principle.name: Be Specific About File Changes
principle.description: List the exact files that will be created, modified, or deleted. Include approximate line ranges for changes.
principle.rationale: Specificity helps users evaluate whether the plan is correct and complete before approval.

principle.name: Identify Dependencies
principle.description: Note any external dependencies, API requirements, or prerequisites that must be in place before implementation begins.
principle.rationale: Unaddressed dependencies cause implementation delays and unexpected roadblocks.

required_sections.section: Overview — one-paragraph summary of what will be built
Objectives — specific goals the plan aims to achieve
Steps — numbered, ordered list of implementation steps
File Changes — which files are created/modified/deleted
Risks — potential issues and mitigation strategies

formatting_guidelines.guideline: Use code blocks for file paths and command examples
Include a todo checklist at the top using standard markdown [-] syntax

pitfall.description: Hanging after research without writing a plan document
pitfall.why_problematic: The user never receives a tangible artifact to review or approve.
pitfall.correct_approach: After gathering context, immediately create the todo list and write the plan file before presenting for review.

pitfall.why_problematic: Implementation mode cannot execute ambiguous instructions reliably.
pitfall.correct_approach: Each step should reference specific files, functions, or components and describe concrete actions.

pitfall.why_problematic: Assumptions about requirements lead to plans that don't match user intent.
pitfall.correct_approach: Ask focused questions when critical details are missing, especially for scope, priorities, or constraints.

category.name: before_presenting_plan
category.item: Plan is saved as a markdown file in docs/
Plan includes all required sections (overview, objectives, steps, files, risks)
Steps are numbered and ordered by dependency
File changes are specific with paths listed
Todo list created using update_todo_list tool

category.name: after_approval
category.item: Handoff message includes the plan file path
Appropriate implementation mode is specified in new_task
User knows what to expect next
